snippet: An employee is entitled to be absent from duty for a total of 7 years’ parental absence following or in conjunction with the birth, adoption or otherwise becoming the legal parent of a child. Excluding partner leave and other paid parental leave, only 1 parental absence may be approved for a particular child (or children in respect of a multiple birth) which must be a continuous absence. Apart from any paid leave taken in conjunction with the birth, adoption or otherwise becoming the legal parent of a child, parental absence is without pay. Parental absence may extend up to the 7th birthday of the child for whom the leave was granted with the following exceptions: If a total of 7 years of parental absence has been taken, a further period of parental absence may be approved in the event of any subsequent birth, adoption or otherwise becoming the legal parent of a child. Where 2 employees are eligible to access parental absence in respect of the same child, the provisions of the National Employment Standards permit both employees to be absent concurrently for a maximum of 8 weeks. During this 8 week period each employee is entitled to take any form of paid leave (including partner, other paid parental leave or long service leave) or unpaid leave.
